title: HTTPS and Secure Websites
drDigitalIntro:
The lock icon means your connection is encrypted — scrambled so nobody between you and the site can read it.
• Not the coffee-shop WiFi owner, not your internet provider.
• But the lock does NOT mean the site itself is safe! A scam site can have a lock too.
• The lock protects the pipe, not what's on the other end.
• Always look for the lock before typing passwords or card numbers.

playgroundTask.goal: Check that a site is secure before trusting it

steps.say: Before typing anything private, check the site is secure. Go to shop.example — type it and press Enter.
See the lock next to the address? Click it to learn what it means.

drDigitalSuccess: You read the lock info and clicked Got it. The lock means your connection is encrypted — but remember, even scam sites can have a lock. It protects the pipe, not the site.
drDigitalHint: Click the lock icon in the address bar, read the explanation, then click Got it.
